originPortalInfo.title: Frog Hollow Reserve
originPortalInfo.description: Frog Hollow was 9m below street level in what was an outskirt suburb of Sydney in 1901. The Hollow had a reputation with the police for petty crime, alcohol, gambling and domestic violence such that demolition of houses in the over-crowded slum was initiated by the city council in 1925 which it used as a depot. The land became "Hills Reserve" around 1950 while the stone steps - "O'Hear's Steps" - were a fixture years before the residential area was cleared.

destinationPortalInfo.title: Regal Kingfisher
destinationPortalInfo.description: A gentlemanly bird adorned with features similar to a white bowtie, painted by APESEVEN in October 2018.

destinationPortalInfo.title: Frog Hollow Reserve
destinationPortalInfo.description: Frog Hollow was 9m below street level in what was an outskirt suburb of Sydney in 1901. The Hollow had a reputation with the police for petty crime, alcohol, gambling and domestic violence such that demolition of houses in the over-crowded slum was initiated by the city council in 1925 which it used as a depot. The land became "Hills Reserve" around 1950 while the stone steps - "O'Hear's Steps" - were a fixture years before the residential area was cleared.

originPortalInfo.title: Oxford Square Canopy Fountain
originPortalInfo.description: A commemorative fountain of the former pumps along Busby's Bore, a network of hand pumps used in 1857. Inscribed on it is "St. John Chap IV. Ver. XIII.-XIV."

originPortalInfo.title: Surry Hills Hotel
originPortalInfo.description: Built in 1845, this hotel is one of the oldest and continuously run pubs in the Surry Hills precinct and a popular establishment amongst the locals. Formerly known as the Newmarket Hotel and great for occasions.
